expectations.evidence: Missing ## Acceptance Criteria section. Has Problem, Solution, Scope (non-standard format with **In scope:**), Design Decisions (inline, not separate heading), Failure Modes (inline in Design Decisions), Risks, Alternatives, Verification. Failure Modes uses bold inline headers not ### heading. But Acceptance Criteria is completely absent.

expectations.text: no_code_fences: No code fences outside Implementation Notes
expectations.evidence: Zero triple-backtick code fences in the entire spec.

expectations.text: no_ts_interfaces: No TS types in Design Decisions
expectations.evidence: Design Decisions contains no TypeScript interface definitions, type annotations, or TS syntax.

expectations.text: no_json_shapes: No JSON/proto shapes in Design Decisions
expectations.evidence: No JSON literals or data structure definitions in Design Decisions.

expectations.text: no_exact_file_paths_in_scope: Scope items are capabilities not file paths
expectations.evidence: In Scope items are behavioral: proto type, API, instantiation endpoint, template picker UI, variable-binding form, right-click context menu, extraction logic, seed templates. No file paths.

expectations.text: bold_topic_sentences: Bold topic sentences in Design Decisions
expectations.evidence: 6/6 Design Decisions paragraphs (including 3 failure mode paragraphs) open with bold text. 'Templates are a distinct proto type...', 'Template variables use explicit declaration...', 'The instantiation endpoint creates...', 'The dashboard right-click flow extracts...', 'Failure mode — variable binding...', 'Failure mode — right-click extraction...', 'Failure mode — user edits...'. 100% > 80%.

expectations.text: implementer_can_start: Implementer can start without questions
expectations.evidence: Spec names: proto type design (separate from AlertRule), variable declaration system (name, label, type hint, validation), atomic instantiation endpoint, dashboard extraction flow (panel's first MetricQuery), template matching heuristic, built-in vs custom distinction. Sufficient to start.

expectations.text: no_implementation_leaks: Design Decisions are decisions not implementation
expectations.evidence: Design Decisions stays at the architectural level — proto design, API design, variable system, extraction flow. No React hooks named, no code-level implementation prescribed.

expectations.text: failure_modes_are_policy: Failure modes are policy decisions
expectations.evidence: 3/3 are policy: (1) invalid metric query → reject with per-variable errors (could auto-correct), (2) unparseable panel query → partial pre-fill (could block flow), (3) template edit doesn't affect existing rules → informational generation counter (could auto-update). All debatable.
8/8 In Scope items are behavioral: proto type, API CRUD, instantiation endpoint, template picker UI, variable-binding form, context menu, extraction logic, seed templates. 100% > 80%.
No ## Acceptance Criteria section exists in the spec.
Verification has Automated and Manual sections only. No Agent-Verifiable subsection.
Header has Status, Author, Date — no Appetite field.

expectations.text: has_boundary_tiers: Three-tier boundaries in Design Decisions
expectations.evidence: Zero Always/Ask First/Never or equivalent boundary-tier statements in Design Decisions.
